365.message: The Microsoft Graph Data Connect app authorization experience is integrated with Microsoft 365 audit logs. When admins approve or deny a Data Connect application, an auditable event emits to Microsoft 365 audit logs with relevant data about what was approved or denied. Use any of the following field

410.message: The requested resource is no longer available at the server.

500.message: There was an internal server error while processing the request.

400.message: In some common scenarios, POST and PATCH requests can return a 400 status code. The following are some of the common causes:
400.description: Open Type properties are not of correct types, or the type isn't specified, or they do not contain any properties. For example, plannerAssignments properties with complex values need to declare @odata.type property with value microsoft.graph.plannerAssignment. Order hint values do not have the correct format. For example, an order hint value is being set directly to the value returned to the client. * The data is logically inconsistent. For example, start date of task is later than due date of t

403.message: In addition to the general errors, the Planner API also returns the 403 status code when a service-defined limit has been exceeded. If this is the case, the code property on the error resource type will indicate the type of the limit exceeded by the request. For details about the limits, see Planner
403.description: | Value | Description | | :---------------------------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | MaximumProjectsOwnedByUser | The maximum number of plans contained by a group limit has been exceeded. This limit applies to plans contained by a group based on the container property of the plannerPlan resource. | | MaximumProjectsShare

409.message: Conflict errors primarily occur when multiple Teams apps installed in the same scope (team, chat, user, and so on) have the same Microsoft Entra appId in the webApplicationInfo section of the manifest. When this happens, you get an error like Found multiple applications with the same Microsoft Entra
409.description: ## Related content - Tutorial: Send activity feed notifications - Best practices for using Microsoft Teams activity feed notifications - Design activity feed notifications for Microsoft Teams - Send activity feed notifications .NET sample - Send activity feed notifications Node.js sample

429.message: Client application has been throttled and shouldn't attempt to repeat the request until an amount of time has elapsed.

notSupported.message: App-only callers aren't supported for SharePoint Embedded containers.

accessDenied.message: The caller doesn't have permission to remove the retention label.

itemNotFound.message: The item can't be found or is no longer accessible.

HttpStatusCode.BadRequest.message: Unsupported headers are present in the request. Two headers aren't supported: If-Modified-Since If-Range

HttpStatusCode.Forbidden.message: Caller isn't authorized to act on behalf of the user or send notification to the user.

HttpStatusCode.MethodNotAllowed.message: The HTTP method used isn't supported.

HttpStatusCode.UnsupportedMediaType.message: The header Content-Encoding is present and has compression algorithm values other than Deflate or Gzip.

HttpStatusCode.Unauthorized.message: Request body contains invalid activity data types.

HttpStatusCode.NotAcceptable.message: Request has been throttled or the server is busy.

badRequest.message: There was an issue with your request. Make sure the registrationId you entered is valid or registered for your tenant.

ActivityGroupIdFromActivitiesTypeValidat.message: ActivityGroupId field in ActivityType must be a subset of ID field in ActivityGroup.
ActivityGroupIdFromActivitiesTypeValidat.description: The ActivityGroupId field in ActivityType isn't a subset of the Id field in ActivityGroup in the app manifest.

ActivityTypesMustExistWithActivitiesGrou.description: The ActivityGroup property is available, but the ActivityType property is missing in the app manifest.

412.message: All Planner API POST, PATCH, and DELETE requests require the If-Match header to be specified with the last known etag value of the resource that is subject to the request. The 412 status code can also be returned if the etag value specified in the request no longer matches a version of the resource

025.message: By default, applications created after August 31, 2024 receive a 403 Unauthorized error when making requests to Azure AD Graph APIs, unless you configure them to allow extended Azure AD Graph access. Additionally, you must configure existing apps created before August 31, 2024 and making requests to
025.description: The following request shows how to update an app to enable extended Azure AD Graph access. The ID used in this example is the object ID of the application, not the application ID. The request returns a 204 No Content response code. #### Option 1

InvalidCreateRequest.message: Can't process the request because it's malformed or incorrect.
InvalidCreateRequest.description: Create request is null, invalid or its size is too large.

InvalidDisplayName.message: Thrown when the policy display name exceeds the character limit or is empty/null.
InvalidDisplayName.description: Please ensure policy name length should be > 0 chars and <= 1024 chars.

InvalidProtectionUnitId.message: Thrown when one of the Protection Unit IDs is invalid.

ProtectionUnitsLimitBreached.message: Thrown when the user tries to add more Protection Units than supported in one request.
ProtectionUnitsLimitBreached.description: Number of Protection Units in each request must not exceed 50.

PolicyCreationNotAllowed.message: Thrown when an active protection policy already exists for the service and the user tries to create a new policy.
PolicyCreationNotAllowed.description: Can't create Policy. Another active Policy already exists.

PolicySaveFailed.message: Thrown when a transient error occurs in the M365 Backup service.
PolicySaveFailed.description: An unknown error occurred. Try again.

InvalidPolicyId.message: Thrown when the Policy ID in request is invalid, null, or empty.

PolicyNotFound.message: Thrown when the ID is valid but the policy doesn't exist.
PolicyNotFound.description: Unable to get the Protection Policy.

ProtectionUnitNotFound.message: This is a delta patch ProtectionUnit level error returned when the user requests to remove Protection Unit, which isn't present in the service.
ProtectionUnitNotFound.description: ProtectionUnit level error: ProtectionUnit doesn't exist.

DuplicateProtectionUnitInList.message: This is a Protection Unit level error returned when the request has duplicate artifacts in the list.
DuplicateProtectionUnitInList.description: Protection Unit level error: Duplicate Protection Unit in list.

ProtectionUnitActionNotAllowed.message: The artifact with the given protection unit ID can't be removed as it's protected by a dynamic rule.
ProtectionUnitActionNotAllowed.description: Protection units protected via dynamic rules can't be removed manually.

PolicyActionNotAllowed.message: Thrown when the policy isn't in Inactive state.
PolicyActionNotAllowed.description: Can't activate policy. The policy isn't in correct state. Make sure the policy is in Inactive state.

FilterLengthExceeded.message: This error occurs when client attempt to send too many filters or queries all at once in a single request including both "and" and "or" filter queries combined.
FilterLengthExceeded.description: Filter length exceeds the allowed limit.

UnknownError.message: Unknown error occurred or a provider-side error.
UnknownError.description: Unknown error occurred. Please try again. If the issue persists, contact support.

InvalidArtifactId.message: Invalid or nonexistent Protection Unit ID.
InvalidArtifactId.description: Protection Unit to remove wasn't found.

QuotaExceeded.message: Exceeding backup size quota (specifically for Exo).
QuotaExceeded.description: You have exceeded the backup quota.

InvalidArtifactRequest.message: Empty or null restore artifact list.
InvalidArtifactRequest.description: There should be at least one restore artifact.

InvalidRestorePoint.description: Restore Point ID is invalid or empty.

DifferentRestoreDestinationType.message: Different destination types for artifacts in request.

RestoreArtifactsLimitBreached.message: Exceeded maximum artifacts limit of 50 for single request.

DuplicateArtifactInList.description: The restore artifact requested is already present.

InvalidRestoreSessionId.message: Restore session ID invalid, null, or empty.

RestoreSessionNotFound.message: Valid ID but session not found.

InvalidPatchRequest.message: Can't process the request because it's malformed or incorrect.
InvalidPatchRequest.description: Can't process the request because it's malformed or incorrect.

RestoreSessionActionNotAllowed.message: Attempt to activate active restore session.
RestoreSessionActionNotAllowed.description: Restore session can only be activated in Draft state.

IncompleteArtifactDeletion.message: Service unable to delete all requested artifacts.
IncompleteArtifactDeletion.description: Unable to delete session, try again.

TooManyActiveRestoreSessions.message: Attempt to activate more than 25 sessions simultaneously.
TooManyActiveRestoreSessions.description: Temporary error: Too many active restore sessions.

ArtifactNotFound.description: The requested restore artifact wasn't found for restore.

InvalidRestoreTime.description: Restore Artifact can't be restored to this timestamp, the restore time provided is invalid or beyond retention period.

ArtifactCannotBeRestored.message: Restore Artifact state doesn't allow restoration.
ArtifactCannotBeRestored.description: The restore artifact is in an unexpected state such as preservation lock or TenantDivestiture which is blocking the restore.

DuplicateArtifactRestoreRequest.message: Concurrent restoration of the same restore artifact.
DuplicateArtifactRestoreRequest.description: An ongoing Restore was found for the given restore artifact. Please wait till its completion and try again.

InvalidInclusionRuleCreateRequest.message: Can't process the request because it's malformed or incorrect.

InvalidRuleExpression.message: Value can't be null or invalid expression.
InvalidRuleExpression.description: The expression isn't valid and can't be parsed.

InvalidRuleExpressionGroupLimitExceeded.message: Rule expression that contains more than 100 group IDs isn't allowed.
InvalidRuleExpressionGroupLimitExceeded.description: The expression contains more group IDs than the imposed limit allows.

InvalidProtectionRulePatchRequest.message: The isAutoApplyEnabled parameter isn't supported in a patch request.
InvalidProtectionRulePatchRequest.description: The isAutoApplyEnabled parameter can be supplied only at the time of creation. The conversion between a static rule and a dynamic rule, and vice versa, isn't allowed.

InvalidProtectionRuleStatusForUpdation.message: Update operation isn't allowed in the current state.
InvalidProtectionRuleStatusForUpdation.description: Invalid state to invoke the update operation.

PatchNotAllowedForStaticRule.message: Patch operation isn't allowed for a static rule.

ProtectionRuleNotFound.message: Protection rule with the given ID doesn't exist.
ProtectionRuleNotFound.description: The given rule ID is either incorrect or the rule was deleted.

InvalidProtectionRuleStatusForDynamicRul.message: Patch operation isn't allowed in the current state.
InvalidProtectionRuleStatusForDynamicRul.description: The patch operation isn't allowed in the deleteRequested state.

InvalidInclusionRuleId.message: Rule ID in the request is invalid, null, or empty.

InvalidProtectionRuleStatusForDynamicRul.message: The deleteAndUnprotect operation isn't allowed in the current state.
InvalidProtectionRuleStatusForDynamicRul.description: Invalid state to invoke the deleteAndUnprotect action.

ProtectionRuleActionNotAllowed.message: Run action isn't allowed for protection rule.
ProtectionRuleActionNotAllowed.description: Invalid state to invoke the run action.

RunNotAllowedForDynamicRule.message: Run operation isn't allowed for dynamic rule.

InvalidProtectionRuleStatusForDeletion.message: Delete operation isn't allowed in the current state.
InvalidProtectionRuleStatusForDeletion.description: Invalid state to invoke the delete operation.

InvalidProtectionRuleStatusForDynamicRul.message: Delete operation isn't allowed in the current state.
InvalidProtectionRuleStatusForDynamicRul.description: Invalid state to invoke the delete operation on a dynamic rule.

InvalidSearchRequest.message: Requested date time limit or protection unit ID is invalid.

UnableToReadBillingProfile.message: Unable to read billing profile from billing profile provider.
UnableToReadBillingProfile.description: Unable to read billing profile from billing profile provider.

InvalidBillingProfile.message: Invalid billing profile received from the provider.
InvalidBillingProfile.description: Invalid billing profile received from the provider.

InvalidAppOwnerTenantId.description: Invalid App owner tenant id received from the user.

InvalidBulkRestoreArtifactId.message: Bulk restore request ID is invalid.

BulkRestoreInvalidCreateRequest.description: The create request is null or invalid.

BulkRestoreInvalidCreateRequestSiteUrl.message: Bulk restore site web URL is invalid.
BulkRestoreInvalidCreateRequestSiteUrl.description: Site URL is null, empty, or in an incorrect format.

BulkRestoreInvalidCreateRequestUserEmail.message: Bulk restore user email is invalid.
BulkRestoreInvalidCreateRequestUserEmail.description: Email is null, empty, or in an incorrect format.

BulkRestoreArtifactsNotFound.message: No bulkRestoreArtifact item with the given bulkRestoreArtifactId.
BulkRestoreArtifactsNotFound.description: No restoreArtifactBulkAdditionRequest associated with the provided bulkRestoreArtifactId in the URI.

InvalidStateForBulkRequestDeletion.message: Validation fails when the service type of the restore session and create request are different.
InvalidStateForBulkRequestDeletion.description: A bulk request can only be deleted when its status is completed or completedWithErrors.

404.message: The following are some reasons why you might encounter a 404 Not Found error:
404.description: Recent call: If the call was made within the last 150 minutes, the call record might not be generated yet. Wait 150 minutes after the call ended and try again. Old call: If the call is older than 30 days, the call records API returns a 404 Not Found error by design. * Other issues: If neither of the reasons apply, check for any outages reported by the call records API team in the Health tab of the Teams Admin portal. Also, you can open a support ticket with the call records API team for assistan

401.message: Make sure that your application is presenting a valid access token to Microsoft Graph as part of the request. This error often means that the access token might be missing in the HTTP authenticate request header or that the token is invalid or is expired. We strongly recommend that you use the Micro

402.message: The payment requirements for the API haven't been met.

405.message: The HTTP method in the request isn't allowed on the resource.

406.message: This service doesn’t support the format requested in the Accept header.

411.message: A Content-Length header is required on the request.

413.message: The request size exceeds the maximum limit.

415.message: The content type of the request is a format that isn't supported by the service.

416.message: The specified byte range is invalid or unavailable.

422.message: Can't process the request because it is semantically incorrect.

423.message: The resource that is being accessed is locked.

503.message: The service is temporarily unavailable for maintenance or is overloaded. You can repeat the request after a delay, the length of which can be specified in a Retry-After header.

504.message: The server, while acting as a proxy, didn't receive a timely response from the upstream server it needed to access in attempting to complete the request.

507.message: The maximum storage quota has been reached.

022.message: 1. In Solution Explorer, choose Connected Services to open the Connected Services tab. 2. In Service Dependencies, click the + button to add a new service dependency. 3. Scroll down and choose Access Microsoft 365 services with Microsoft Graph.

001.message: The request is missing the required "Presentation" part. Exactly one is required.

012.message: Either remove the check-out requirement from the library, or move the notebook.

140.description: ``html To-do note tag in completed state (checked box in the UI) `` See Use note tags.

166.message: For more information, see Microsoft Graph service-specific throttling guidance.

006.message: The OAuth token doesn't have the required scopes to perform the requested action. Specifically the edit permission. See Notes permissions.

009.description: - Microsoft Graph error responses and resource types - OneNote API reference

forbidden.message: The caller doesn't have permission to perform the action.

conflict.message: The current state conflicts with what the request expects.

payloadTooLarge.message: The request size exceeds the maximum limit.

badGateway.message: The server encountered a temporary error and couldn't complete your request.

gatewayTimeout.message: The server, while acting as a proxy, didn't receive a timely response from the upstream server in order to complete the request.
